title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Kingsbridge, Bronx | $900 – $2,500 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Kingsbridge, Bronx. Safe, humane bat control for homes and buildings. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: Bats in your Kingsbridge attic or walls? Best Pest Control NYC provides safe, humane bat removal and exclusion services throughout the Kingsbridge neighborhood of the Bronx. Our licensed wildlife specialists use proven methods to remove bats and seal entry points, protecting your home from future infestations.
whyUs: With over a decade serving Kingsbridge residents, we understand the unique challenges of bat problems in pre-war buildings and residential homes throughout this historic Bronx neighborhood. Our team provides guaranteed exclusion work, thorough attic inspections, and cleanup services to ensure your property remains bat-free year-round.
localContext: Kingsbridge's mix of classic apartment buildings, single-family homes near Van Cortlandt Park, and older construction along Broadway creates ideal conditions for bat colonies to roost in attics, chimneys, and wall voids. The proximity to green spaces like the park and Jerome Park Reservoir attracts bat populations that often seek shelter in nearby residential structures during warmer months.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Kingsbridge, Bronx?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Kingsbridge typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and the complexity of your building's structure. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before any work begins.

faqs.question: Is bat removal in NYC legal and humane?
faqs.answer: Yes. We follow all NYC and New York State regulations for bat removal, using only humane exclusion methods that allow bats to leave naturally while preventing re-entry. We never use poisons or harmful traps, and we time our work to avoid bat maternity season (May-August) when flightless pups are present.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my Kingsbridge home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, bat droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, brown staining around roof gaps or vents, and seeing bats entering or exiting your home at sunset. Bats often enter through gaps as small as 3/8 of an inch in older Kingsbridge buildings.

faqs.question: What does the bat exclusion process involve?
faqs.answer: Our process includes a thorough inspection to locate all entry points and assess colony size, installation of one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to exit but not return, sealing all secondary entry points, a waiting period to ensure all bats have left, removal of exclusion devices and final sealing, and optional attic cleanup and decontamination to remove guano and restore insulation.
